evals.prompt: I want to trace my DSPy pipeline locally so I can see what prompts are being sent and how many tokens each call uses.
evals.expected_output: Sets up Phoenix with local mode and auto-instrumentation for DSPy tracing

assertions.name: installs_all_packages
assertions.description: Installs arize-phoenix AND openinference-instrumentation-dspy AND openinference-instrumentation-litellm

assertions.name: calls_launch_app
assertions.description: Calls px.launch_app() to start the local UI before register()

assertions.name: uses_register_auto_instrument
assertions.description: Uses register(auto_instrument=True) from phoenix.otel, not the legacy DSPyInstrumentor pattern

assertions.name: shows_dspy_lm_config
assertions.description: Shows how to configure a DSPy LM and run a traced call

assertions.name: mentions_localhost
assertions.description: Mentions the UI is available at localhost:6006

evals.prompt: My team wants to use Phoenix in the cloud so everyone can see DSPy traces. How do I set that up?
evals.expected_output: Configures Phoenix for cloud mode with Arize platform

assertions.name: sets_collector_endpoint
assertions.description: Sets PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT to the Arize cloud URL

assertions.name: no_launch_app_for_cloud
assertions.description: Does not call px.launch_app() for cloud mode, or explains it is not needed

evals.prompt: I have traces in Phoenix and want to automatically score whether my DSPy outputs are helpful. How do I use Phoenix evals?
evals.expected_output: Uses Phoenix evals module to classify traced outputs

assertions.name: imports_llm_classify
assertions.description: Imports llm_classify and OpenAIModel from phoenix.evals

assertions.name: gets_spans_dataframe
assertions.description: Gets spans as a DataFrame using px.Client().get_spans_dataframe()

assertions.name: defines_template
assertions.description: Defines a template with column placeholders for evaluation

assertions.name: uses_rails_param
assertions.description: Uses rails parameter to constrain classification labels
